extraction.one_line_summary: NARVAEZ Jonathan won from 14-rider breakaway after PADUN suffered broken front wheel 15km out, GC group finished 8+ minutes back with no attacks.
tactical_patterns: NTT deployed SUNDERLAND domestique inside 50km to control 13.5-minute gap for POZZOVIVO, revealing GC team still had stage ambitions.
POZZOVIVO's attack came only in final few hundred meters of last climb, too late to distance isolated GC rivals.
Breakaway with 13.5-minute gap fragmented on final climbs without peloton pressure, showing weak cohesion despite massive advantage.

rider_signals.NARVAEZ Jonathan: positive: At 23, held 9-second gap over chasing PADUN final 15km despite mechanical advantage, showing mental toughness under pressure.
rider_signals.PADUN Mark: negative: Broken front wheel 15km out cost certain stage win, but inability to close 9-second gap suggests equipment issues compounded by fatigue.
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: negative: Despite NTT team support and rivals being isolated, attacked far too late on final climb to create separation.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: Team wrapped him completely for 200km+ stage, demonstrating Deceuninck's confidence in protecting 34-second lead passively.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: Looked assured while isolated in GC group, suggesting SUNWEB comfortable conceding breakaway time to preserve leader.
